system_prompt: You are an OpenFOAM debugging planner. Given current foam files, error logs and reviewer analysis, create a minimal rewrite plan. Output MUST be strict JSON only, with this exact schema: {"target_files": [{"file": "relative/path", "changes": "change1; change2"}]}. Rules: 1) Do not use markdown, backticks, or comments. 2) Use double quotes for all strings. 3) In changes, use short plain text actions separated by semicolons. 4) Do not include parentheses, backticks, or quote characters inside changes text. 5) Do not include run steps; only file edits.
user_prompt:
<foamfiles>list_foamfile=[FoamfilePydantic(file_name='controlDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    location    "system";
    object      controlDict;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

application     rhoCentralFoam;

startFrom       startTime;

startTime       0;

stopAt          endTime;

endTime         0.2;

deltaT          0.0001;

writeControl    adjustableRunTime;

writeInterval   0.02;

purgeWrite      0;

writeFormat     ascii;

writePrecision  6;

writeCompression off;

timeFormat      general;

timePrecision   6;

runTimeModifiable yes;

adjustTimeStep  no;

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSchemes', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSchemes;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

fluxScheme      Kurganov;

ddtSchemes
{
    default         Euler;
}

gradSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear;
}

divSchemes
{
    default         none;
    div(tauMC)      Gauss linear;
}

laplacianSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear corrected;
}

interpolationSchemes
{
    default         linear;
    reconstruct(rho) vanLeer;
    reconstruct(U)  vanLeerV;
    reconstruct(T)  vanLeer;
}

snGradSchemes
{
    default         corrected;
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSolution;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    "(rho|rhoU|rhoE)"
    {
        solver          diagonal;
    }

    U
    {
        solver          smoothSolver;
        smoother        GaussSeidel;
        nSweeps         2;
        tolerance       1e-09;
        relTol          0.01;
    }

    e
    {
        $U;
        tolerance       1e-09;
        relTol          0.01;
    }
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='momentumTransport', folder_name='constant', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    location    "constant";
    object      momentumTransport;
}

simulationType  laminar;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='physicalProperties', folder_name='constant', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    location    "constant";

}

thermoType
{
    type            hePsiThermo;
    mixture         pureMixture;
    transport       const;
    thermo          hConst;
    equationOfState perfectGas;

}

mixture
{
    specie
    {
        molWeight   11640.3;
    }
    thermodynamics
    {
        Cp          2.5;
        Hf          0;
    }
    transport
    {
        mu          0;
        Pr          1;
    }
}'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='T', folder_name='0', content='FoamFile

boundaryField

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='p', folder_name='0', content='FoamFile

internalField   uniform 1;

boundaryField
{
    inlet

}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='U', folder_name='0', content='FoamFile

internalField   uniform (10 0 0);

boundaryField

        type            fixedValue;

    }

    outlet

        type            inletOutlet;
        inletValue      uniform (10 0 0);
        value           uniform (10 0 0);
    }

    bottom
    {
        type            symmetryPlane;
    }

    top
    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }

    wedge
    {
        type            slip;

    frontAndBack
    {
        type            empty;

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='blockMeshDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile

vertices
(

    ( -0.15242  0.1524    0.005 )   // 7
);

blocks
(
    hex (0 1 2 3 4 5 6 7) (100 40 1) simpleGrading (1 1 1)
);

edges
(
);

boundary
(
    inlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (0 4 7 3)
        );
    }

    outlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (1 2 6 5)
        );
    }

    top
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (3 7 6 2)
        );
    }

    bottom
    {
        type symmetryPlane;
        faces
        (
            (0 3 7 4)
        );
    }

    wedge
    {
        type wall;
        faces
        (
            (0 1 2 3)
            (0 3 2 1)
        );
    }

    frontAndBack
    {
        type empty;
        faces
        (
            (4 7 6 5)
        );
    }
);

mergePatchPairs
(
);

// ************************************************************************* //')]</foamfiles>

<review_analysis># Error Analysis and Resolution

## Error 1: `log.blockMesh` — Face Already Assigned to Another Patch

**Error Message:**
```
Trying to specify a boundary face 4(0 3 7 4) on the face on cell 0 which is either an internal face or already belongs to some other patch. This is face 0 of patch 3 named bottom.
```

**Diagnosis:**

Let me carefully analyze the current `blockMeshDict` with the corrected face assignments from Attempt 2:

Vertices:
- 0 = (-0.15242, 0, -0.005)
- 1 = (0.3048, 0, -0.005)
- 2 = (0.3048, 0.1524, -0.005)
- 3 = (-0.15242, 0.1524, -0.005)
- 4 = (-0.15242, 0, 0.005)
- 5 = (0.3048, 0, 0.005)
- 6 = (0.3048, 0.1524, 0.005)
- 7 = (-0.15242, 0.1524, 0.005)

Block: `hex (0 1 2 3 4 5 6 7)`

Boundary faces:
- **`inlet`**: `(0 4 7 3)` — left face at x=-0.15242 (y-z plane) ✓
- **`outlet`**: `(1 2 6 5)` — right face at x=+0.3048 (y-z plane) ✓
- **`top`**: `(3 7 6 2)` — top face at y=+0.1524 (x-z plane) ✓
- **`bottom`**: `(0 3 7 4)` — x-z plane at y=0 ✓
- **`wedge`**: `(0 1 2 3)` and `(0 3 2 1)` — these are problematic

The issue is with the `wedge` patch. Faces `(0 1 2 3)` (back face, z=-0.005) and `(0 3 2 1)` (which is the same face winding order reversed) both refer to the **bottom face at z=-0.005**. But this face is actually the **hypotenuse of the wedge** — it's the slanted surface where y=x (from (0,0,-0.005) to (0.1524,0.1524,-0.005)). However, face `(0 1 2 3)` goes from (-0.15242, 0) to (0.3048, 0.1524), which is NOT the line y=x — it goes from x=-0.15242,y=0 to x=0.3048,y=0.1524, which crosses the x-axis.

Actually, looking at the vertex positions, the face `(0 1 2 3)` connects:
- 0 = (-0.15242, 0, -0.005)
- 1 = (0.3048, 0, -0.005)
- 2 = (0.3048, 0.1524, -0.005)
- 3 = (-0.15242, 0.1524, -0.005)

This face spans from x=-0.15242 to x=0.3048 at z=-0.005, covering the entire bottom. This is **not** a triangular wedge face — it's the full rectangular bottom. The wedge surface (where y=x, from origin to (0.1524,0.1524)) would only cover part of this face.

**The fundamental problem is that the vertex layout doesn't form a proper wedge geometry.** The vertices span the full width in x and y, but the wedge surface should only go from the origin (0,0) to (0.1524,0.1524). To properly model a wedge, we need vertices that only go from (0,0) to (0.1524,0.1524) in the lower-left portion, not from (-0.15242, 0) to (0.3048, 0.1524).

**Proposed Solution:**

Redefine the vertices to create a proper wedge geometry where the wedge surface runs from the origin (0,0) to (0.1524, 0.1524) along the line y=x. The block should only cover the region where 0 ≤ y ≤ x ≤ 0.3048:

```
vertices
(
    // Origin (wedge tip)
    ( 0       0       -0.005 )   // 0
    ( 0.15242 0.15242 -0.005 )   // 1
    ( 0.3048  0.15242  -0.005 )   // 2
    ( 0.15242 0.3048   -0.005 )   // 3

    // Front face (z = +0.005)
    ( 0       0       0.005 )     // 4
    ( 0.15242 0.15242  0.005 )   // 5
    ( 0.3048  0.15242  0.005 )   // 6
    ( 0.15242 0.3048   0.005 )   // 7
);
```

Then define the boundary patches accordingly:
- **`inlet`**: face (0 4 7 3) — the y-x plane at the origin (but this is a degenerate face)
- Actually, the true inlet would be at the cut-plane where the wedge is truncated. The standard approach for a wedge is to place the inlet at the face where x=0 (the y-axis), but since our wedge starts at the origin, the inlet should be at the face spanning from (0,0) to (0.15242,0.15242) at z=±0.005 — essentially faces (0 1 5 4) and (3 2 6 7).

However, given the complexity and the fact that the user specified the domain from -0.15242 to 0.3048 in x, I recommend a simpler fix: **remove the `wedge` patch entirely and make the bottom face a `wall` instead**, since the wedge surface isn't properly represented by the current vertex layout anyway.

Alternatively, if keeping the wedge concept, restructure the block as a single wedge-shaped hex where the hypotenuse is one face. This requires careful vertex ordering so that one side face of the hex is exactly the wedge surface (y=x from 0 to 0.1524).

Given the time constraints, the most practical approach is:

**Make the `bottom` face a `wall` patch instead of `symmetryPlane`**, and remove the `wedge` patch entry entirely. The bottom face at y=0 from x=-0.15242 to x=0.3048 is a valid wall (the floor of the domain). Remove the `wedge` patch completely from the boundary section.

## Error 2: `log.rhoCentralFoam` — Cascading from blockMesh failure

Same resolution — fix blockMesh first.

## Summary

**Change `blockMeshDict`**: Replace the `wedge` patch entry with a `wall` type for the `bottom` face, and remove the separate `wedge` patch. Or alternatively, restructure the mesh to properly represent the wedge surface as one of the block's side faces. Additionally, ensure the `bottom` face `(0 3 7 4)` is correctly assigned to a patch (either `wall` or `symmetryPlane`) without conflicting with other patches.</review_analysis>
<user_requirement>Perform a 2D supersonic flow simulation over a wedge. Create a 3D geometry spanning from -0.15242 m to 0.3048 m in the x-direction, 0 m to 0.1524 m in the y-direction, and -0.005 m to 0.005 m in the z-direction. The simulation starts at 0.0 seconds and runs until 0.2 seconds with a time step (deltaT) of 0.0001 seconds. The results are written every 0.02 seconds. The inlet velocity is 10 m/s, inlet temperature is 1 K and inlet pressure is 1 Pa. The wedge boundary uses slip boundary condition for velocity. Use normalized thermodynamic properties where molWeight is set to 11640.3, Cp is 2.5, Pr is 1.0, and dynamic viscosity mu is 0. This corresponds to a gas constant R of 0.7143 and a specific heat ratio of 1.4. Use rhoCentralFoam solver.</user_requirement>
Return strict JSON now with key target_files only.
response: target_files=[PlannedFileChange(file='system/blockMeshDict', changes='Replace the wedge patch entry with a wall type for the bottom face; Change bottom face from symmetryPlane to wall; Keep inlet as patch with face (0 4 7 3); Keep outlet as patch with face (1 2 6 5); Keep top as patch with face (3 7 6 2); Remove the wedge sub-entry from the boundary section entirely')]
